reasoning.typography: Standard sans-serif system with no character. Generic weight distribution, pedestrian hierarchy. Could be any SaaS product. Zero typographic identity beyond 'we picked a safe font'.
reasoning.hierarchy: Hero headline dominates appropriately, product screenshot has reasonable visual weight. But below the fold it collapses into typical feature-grid sameness. One-thing-per-screen discipline breaks down quickly.
reasoning.distinctiveness: Complete stencil. 'Meet the [product category]' headline template, benefit bullets, logo parade, demo CTA. Swap 'Notion' for any B2B SaaS and nothing breaks. The mascot illustrations are the only personality attempt.
reasoning.color: Navy + bright blue CTA shows some restraint. Accent orange on icons is disciplined. Background gradient is tasteful. Better than average — actual palette discipline vs. rainbow vomit.
reasoning.density: Confident use of space in hero area. Product screenshot has dense content that shows real use. Better than the typical 'hero image + three feature cards with acres of padding' syndrome.
reasoning.voice: Structure is pure SaaS stencil: Hero/Social Proof/Features/CTA. 'Meet the night shift' attempts personality but IA is completely conventional. Currently/Selected/Testimonials pattern in different clothes.

winning_patterns: Product screenshot shows actual dense UI with real content/tasks — demonstrates rather than decorates
Color palette has restraint — navy base with controlled accent usage
Confident content density in the product preview area
Mascot illustrations attempt to inject personality into standard template
anti_patterns: Complete headline stencil: 'Meet the [X]' is LinkedIn/Notion/every-rebrand cliché
Logo parade social proof is pure commodity signaling
Zero typographic identity — could be any product using system sans
IA is stencil B2B SaaS despite personality attempts in copy
Feature section below hero collapses into generic three-column grid pattern
